response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack and backend engineer with a strong testing culture and broad experience, but falls critically short of the role's core requirements. The job demands 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum; the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments and a university thesis project — nowhere near the threshold. The candidate's primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which are irrelevant to this C++-centric cloud compute platform role. Additionally, there is no demonstrated technical leadership of cloud or infrastructure teams, no cloud architecture ownership, and no production-level distributed systems work at the scale or depth the role implies.
strengths: Strong testing and software craftsmanship culture (TDD advocacy, workshops, open-source test tooling in DLang) demonstrates engineering discipline.
Some exposure to distributed systems and high-throughput environments (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, micro-services, async I/O).
Broad polyglot background and ability to operate across the full stack signals adaptability and systems thinking.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + a thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum requirement — the single most disqualifying gap.
No evidence of technical leadership over cloud, compute, or infrastructure teams; mentoring is confined to community workshops and code katas, not organizational engineering leadership.

Cloud and infrastructure footprint is shallow (AWS CDK at one role, Kubernetes for a side project) and does not constitute the 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or compute architecture experience required.
